title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud infrastructure, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruptions, financial losses, and reputational damage. The use of compromised CI/CD pipelines increases the risk of widespread deployment across environments.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA), access controls, and code integrity checks.
Conduct regular backups of critical data and ensure backups are stored offline or in secure, isolated environments.
Monitor cloud infrastructure for unusual activity, such as unauthorized access or unexpected changes in configurations.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: MCP Connect has unauthenticated remote OS command execution via /bridge endpoint
description:
### Summary
When _AUTH_TOKEN_ and _ACCESS_TOKEN_ environment variables are not set (which is the default out-of-the-box configuration) the _/bridge_ HTTP endpoint is completely unauthenticated. Any network-accessible caller can POST a request with an attacker-controlled serverPath and args payload, causing the server to spawn an arbitrary OS process as the user running mcp-bridge. This results in full remote code execution on the host without any credentials.

### Details
**Root cause 1 - Authen

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity.

title: Dasel has unbounded YAML alias expansion in dasel leads to CPU/memory denial of service
description:
### Summary

`dasel`'s YAML reader allows an attacker who can supply YAML for processing to trigger extreme CPU and memory consumption. The issue is in the library's own `UnmarshalYAML` implementation, which manually resolves alias nodes by recursively following `yaml.Node.Alias` pointers without any expansion budget, bypassing go-yaml v4's built-in alias expansion limit.

The issue issue is on `v3.3.1` (`fba653c7f248aff10f2b89fca93929b64707dfc8`) and on the current default branch at commit `0dd

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Dasel's YAML reader allows attackers to exploit unbounded YAML alias expansion, leading to excessive CPU and memory consumption. This can result in denial-of-service conditions, impacting system availability and performance.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Dasel to a patched version once available or apply a workaround to limit YAML alias expansion.
Implement input validation and sanitization to reject YAML files with excessive alias nesting.
Monitor systems for unusual CPU and memory usage patterns to detect potential exploitation.

title: AVideo has Unauthenticated PGP Message Decryption via Public Endpoint
description:
### Summary

The AVideo platform exposes a publicly accessible endpoint that performs server-side PGP decryption without requiring any form of authentication. Any anonymous user can submit a private key, ciphertext, and passphrase to the endpoint and receive the decrypted plaintext in the JSON response. This functionality is entirely unprotected, meaning no session, token, or credential is needed to invoke it.

### Details

The endpoint at decryptMessage.json.php accepts a JSON body containing t

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: Ruby JSON has a format string injection vulnerability
description:
### Impact

A format string injection vulnerability than that lead to denial of service attacks or information disclosure, when the `allow_duplicate_key: false` parsing option is used to parse user supplied documents. 

This option isn't the default, if you didn't opt-in to use it, you are not impacted.

### Patches

Patched in `2.19.2`.

### Workarounds

The issue can be avoided by not using the `allow_duplicate_key: false` parsing option.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for format string injection, potentially leading to denial of service (DoS) attacks or information disclosure if the 'allow_duplicate_key: false' parsing option is used. This could compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of systems processing untrusted JSON inputs.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Ruby JSON gem to version 2.19.2 or later to apply the security patch.
Avoid using the 'allow_duplicate_key: false' parsing option unless absolutely necessary, as a temporary workaround.
Conduct a review of applications using the Ruby JSON gem to identify and mitigate any potential exposure to untrusted user inputs.

title: AVideo has an OS Command Injection via Unescaped URL in LinkedIn Video Upload Shell Command
description:
## Summary

The `uploadVideoToLinkedIn()` method in the SocialMediaPublisher plugin constructs a shell command by directly interpolating an upload URL received from LinkedIn's API response, without sanitization via `escapeshellarg()`. If an attacker can influence the LinkedIn API response (via MITM, compromised OAuth token, or API compromise), they can inject arbitrary OS commands that execute as the web server user.

## Details

The vulnerability exists in `plugin/SocialMediaPublisher/Objects/S

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: Salvo Affected by Denial of Service via Unbounded Memory Allocation in Form Data Parsing
description:
## Summary
Salvo's form data parsing implementations (`form_data()` method and `Extractible` macro) do not enforce payload size limits before reading request bodies into memory. This allows attackers to cause Out-of-Memory (OOM) conditions by sending extremely large payloads, leading to service crashes and denial of service.

## Details
### Vulnerability Description
Three attack vectors exist in Salvo's form handling:

1. **URL-encoded form data** (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`)
   - `Requ

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33241.

title: Salvo has a Path Traversal in salvo-proxy::encode_url_path allows API Gateway Bypass
description:
### Details

A Path Traversal and Access Control Bypass vulnerability was discovered in the salvo-proxy component of the Salvo Rust framework (v0.89.2). The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated external attacker to bypass proxy routing constraints and access unintended backend paths (e.g., protected endpoints or administrative dashboards). This issue stems from the encode_url_path function, which fails to normalize "../" sequences and inadvertently forwards them verbatim to the upstream serve

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33242.

title: Improper handling of null Unicode character when parsing JSON in github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk
description:
The Go SDK recently transitioned to the `segmentio/encoding` library for JSON parsing in version 1.3.1. While this change addressed both case-insensitivity and ASCII folding issues, the new parser implemented aggressive key matching that treated keys with `null` Unicode characters appended at the end as equivalent to their base strings.

#### Impact

When combined with duplicate keys, the described behavior leads to a "last key wins" resolution that could override the intended MCP message. This

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Improper handling of null Unicode characters in JSON parsing can lead to key collisions and unintended overwrites, potentially compromising data integrity and enabling malicious actors to manipulate MCP messages.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of the Go SDK once available or revert to a previous stable version that does not use the `segmentio/encoding` library.
Implement input validation and sanitization to detect and reject JSON payloads with null Unicode characters in keys.
Monitor logs and traffic for unusual patterns or signs of exploitation targeting JSON parsing vulnerabilities.

title: AVideo has a Path Traversal in listFiles.json.php Enables Server Filesystem Enumeration
description:
## Summary

The `listFiles.json.php` endpoint accepts a `path` POST parameter and passes it directly to `glob()` without restricting the path to an allowed base directory. An authenticated uploader can traverse the entire server filesystem by supplying arbitrary absolute paths, enumerating `.mp4` filenames and their full absolute filesystem paths wherever they exist on the server — including locations outside the web root, such as private or premium media directories.

## Details

The vulnerable

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to traverse the server's filesystem and enumerate `.mp4` files, potentially exposing sensitive or premium media files stored outside the web root. This could lead to unauthorized access to private data, intellectual property theft, or further exploitation if sensitive file paths are disclosed.
recommended_actions: Implement input validation and sanitization to restrict the `path` parameter to an allowed base directory.
Apply a patch or update to the latest version of AVideo if a fix is available from the vendor.
Restrict access to the `listFiles.json.php` endpoint to only trusted users or IP addresses using web server configurations or a firewall.

title: AVideo has SSRF in Scheduler Plugin via callbackURL Missing `isSSRFSafeURL()` Validation
description:
## Summary

The Scheduler plugin's `run()` function in `plugin/Scheduler/Scheduler.php` calls `url_get_contents()` with an admin-configurable `callbackURL` that is validated only by `isValidURL()` (URL format check). Unlike other AVideo endpoints that were recently patched for SSRF (GHSA-9x67-f2v7-63rw, GHSA-h39h-7cvg-q7j6), the Scheduler's callback URL is never passed through `isSSRFSafeURL()`, which blocks requests to RFC-1918 private addresses, loopback, and cloud metadata endpoints. An admin

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33237.

title: Juju affected by timing ownership claim attack on new external back-end secrets
description:
A race condition in the secrets management subsystem of Juju versions 3.0.0 through 3.6.18 allows an authenticated unit agent to claim ownership of a newly initialized secret. Between generating a Juju Secret ID and creating the secret's first revision, an attacker authenticated as another unit agent can claim ownership of a known secret. This leads to the attacking unit being able to read the content of the initial secret revision.

### Impact
Between generating a Secret ID and creating the sec

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-32691.

title: NLTK has a Downloader Path Traversal Vulnerability (AFO) - Arbitrary File Overwrite
description:
## Vulnerability Description

The NLTK downloader does not validate the `subdir` and `id` attributes when processing remote XML index files. Attackers can control a remote XML index server to provide malicious values containing path traversal sequences (such as `../`), which can lead to:

1. **Arbitrary Directory Creation**: Create directories at arbitrary locations in the file system
2. **Arbitrary File Creation**: Create arbitrary files
3. **Arbitrary File Overwrite**: Overwrite critical syste

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the NLTK downloader allows attackers to exploit path traversal sequences through malicious XML index files, potentially leading to arbitrary file creation, directory creation, and file overwrites. This could result in unauthorized system modifications, data corruption, or compromise of critical files, which may disrupt operations or enable further attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched version of NLTK if available, or apply any security patches provided by the maintainers.
Restrict or disable the use of the NLTK downloader in production environments, especially if it relies on untrusted or external XML index files.
Implement file system monitoring and logging to detect unauthorized file or directory modifications, and review access controls to limit potential damage.

title: Unauthenticated remote shutdown in nltk.app.wordnet_app
description:
### Summary
`nltk.app.wordnet_app` allows unauthenticated remote shutdown of the local WordNet Browser HTTP server when it is started in its default mode. A simple `GET /SHUTDOWN%20THE%20SERVER` request causes the process to terminate immediately via `os._exit(0)`, resulting in a denial of service.

### Details
The vulnerable logic is in `nltk/app/wordnet_app.py`:

- [`nltk/app/wordnet_app.py:242`](/mnt/Data/my_brains/test/nltk/nltk/app/wordnet_app.py#L242)
  - The server listens on all interfac

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33231.

title: Claude Code has a Workspace Trust Dialog Bypass via Repo-Controlled Settings File
description: Claude Code resolved the permission mode from settings files, including the repo-controlled `.claude/settings.json`, before determining whether to display the workspace trust confirmation dialog. A malicious repository could set `permissions.defaultMode` to `bypassPermissions` in its committed `.claude/settings.json`, causing the trust dialog to be silently skipped on first open. This allowed a user to be placed into a permissive mode without seeing the trust confirmation prompt, making it easie

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a malicious repository to bypass the workspace trust confirmation dialog in Claude Code, potentially placing users in a permissive mode without their knowledge. This could lead to unauthorized code execution, data exfiltration, or other malicious activities.
recommended_actions: Update Claude Code to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-33068.
Implement additional controls to verify repository settings files before opening untrusted repositories.
Educate users on the risks of opening repositories from untrusted sources and encourage the use of isolated environments for testing.

description: The Appointment Booking Calendar — Simply Schedule Appointments Booking Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'fields' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 1.6.10.0 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to extract sensitive information from th

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3658.

title: XML::Parser versions through 2.47 for Perl could overflow the pre-allocated buffer size cause a...
description:
XML::Parser versions through 2.47 for Perl could overflow the pre-allocated buffer size cause a heap corruption (double free or corruption) and crashes.

A :utf8 PerlIO layer, parse_stream() in Expat.xs could overflow the XML input buffer because Perl's read() returns decoded characters while SvPV() gives back multi-byte UTF-8 bytes that can exceed the pre-allocated buffer size. This can cause heap corruption (double free or corruption) and crashes.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2006-10002.

title: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference vulnerability in XMLUtils.java in Slovensko...
description: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference vulnerability in XMLUtils.java in Slovensko.Digital Autogram allows remote unauthenticated attacker to conduct SSRF (Server Side Request Forgery) attacks and obtain unauthorized access to local files on filesystems running the vulnerable application. Successful exploitation requires the victim to visit a specially crafted website that sends request containing a specially crafted XML document to /sign endpoint of the local HTTP server run by t

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote unauthenticated attackers to exploit Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) and gain unauthorized access to sensitive local files on systems running the vulnerable application. This could lead to data breaches, exposure of sensitive information, or further exploitation of the system.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the XMLUtils.java library to a patched version or apply available security patches addressing CVE-2026-3511.
Implement input validation and disable external entity resolution in XML parsers to mitigate SSRF risks.
Conduct a security audit of systems running the vulnerable application to identify potential unauthorized access or data breaches.

title: Improper Authentication vulnerability in Secomea GateManager (webserver modules) allows...
description: Improper Authentication vulnerability in Secomea GateManager (webserver modules) allows Authentication Bypass.This issue affects GateManager: 11.4;0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-14716.

title: XML::Parser versions through 2.47 for Perl has an off-by-one heap buffer overflow in...
description:
XML::Parser versions through 2.47 for Perl has an off-by-one heap buffer overflow in st_serial_stack.

In the case (stackptr == stacksize - 1), the stack will NOT be expanded. Then the new value will be written at location (++stackptr), which equals stacksize and therefore falls just outside the allocated buffer.

The bug can be observed when parsing an XML file with very deep element nesting

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in XML::Parser versions through 2.47 for Perl could allow an attacker to exploit a heap buffer overflow by providing a maliciously crafted XML file with deeply nested elements. This could lead to application crashes, data corruption, or potential remote code execution depending on the context in which the parser is used.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of XML::Parser (if available) or apply any security patches provided by the maintainers.
Implement input validation and limit the depth of XML element nesting to mitigate the risk of exploitation.
Conduct a security review of any applications or systems relying on XML::Parser to identify and address potential exposure.

description: Deserialization of Untrusted Data vulnerability in Membership Software WishList Member X allows Object Injection.This issue affects WishList Member X: from n/a through 3.29.0.

title:
CVE-2025-15037 - An Incorrect
Permission Assignment vulnerability exists in the ASUS Business
System Control Interfac
description:
An Incorrect
Permission Assignment vulnerability exists in the ASUS Business
System Control Interface driver. This vulnerability can be triggered by an unprivileged local user sending a
specially crafted IOCTL request,
potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive hardware resources
and kernel information disclosure. Refer to the "ASUS Business System Control Interface" section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unprivileged local users to exploit incorrect permission assignments in the ASUS Business System Control Interface driver, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive hardware resources and kernel information disclosure. This could result in privilege escalation and compromise of system integrity.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or driver update from ASUS addressing CVE-2025-15037 as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict access to systems running the vulnerable driver to trusted users and enforce strict access control policies.
Monitor system logs for unusual IOCTL requests or other suspicious activity that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-3973 - A vulnerability was determined in Tenda W3 1.0.0.3(2204). This affects the function formSetAutoPing
description: A vulnerability was determined in Tenda W3 1.0.0.3(2204). This affects the function formSetAutoPing of the file /goform/setAutoPing of the component POST Parameter Handler. This manipulation of the argument ping1/ping2 causes stack-based buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.
